t_ch: 他们把自己的一切恶行标榜为正义，以至于能与「同谐」的愿景几乎混为一谈。
t_en: They label all of their evil deeds as justice and start believing it is basically the same as Harmony's vision.

t_ch: 稍有疏忽，不忠的种子就会在家族里生根发芽…要撕破他们的伪装，让他们露出丑恶的真面目，可不是个容易活。
t_en: If we aren't vigilant, the seeds of disloyalty will take root in The Family... Ripping off their disguise and exposing the truth of their wickedness is easier said than done, though.

t_ch: 他往这边看了——让你久等了，老同学，这是你要的苏乐达。
t_en: He's looking this way. Sorry to keep you waiting, dear classmate. Here's the SoulGlad you wanted.

t_ch: 黑爪女士一边递给你苏乐达，一边望向远方。她的嘴角扬起一丝不易察觉的笑意。
t_en: Lady Black Claw hands you the SoulGlad while gazing into the distance. A nearly imperceptible smile crosses her lips.

t_ch: 想要借「秩序」来实现自己私欲的，往往没法掀起太大的波澜。真正可怕的，是那些自以为在救赎世人的家伙。
t_en: If these people are using Order to achieve their own desires, then they can never amount to anything. The truly scary ones are those who think they are saving the world.

t_ch: 他们把自己的一切恶行标榜为正义，以至于能与「同谐」的愿景几乎混为一谈。
t_en: They label all of their evil deeds as justice and start believing it is basically the same as Harmony's vision.

t_ch: 稍有疏忽，不忠的种子就会在家族里生根发芽…要撕破他们的伪装，让他们露出丑恶的真面目，可不是个容易活。
t_en: If we aren't vigilant, the seeds of disloyalty will take root in The Family... Ripping off their disguise and exposing the truth of their wickedness is easier said than done, though.

t_ch: 不过…等抓了最后这只老鼠，暂时应该能清净一阵子了，呵呵。
t_en: But... once I catch the very last rat, everything should stay clean for a while, hehe.

t_ch: 你是偷偷溜进来的宾客？怎样，实物比参观手册上的厉害多了吧！
t_en: Did you sneak in here? Well, what do you think? The real thing is much more impressive than what you see in the manual, right?

t_ch: 这里不仅是匹诺康尼最著名的地标性建筑，在梦境的发展历史中也有着重要地位。
t_en: Not only is this place Penacony's most iconic architectural landmark, but it also has an important place in the history of the Dreamscape's development.

t_ch: 哪怕是美梦剧团的广告牌，谈到它时也会骄傲地亮起灯来——能来大剧院一趟，也算是我的梦想。
t_en: Even the Sweet Dreams Troupe's billboard lights up when it talks about this building. Visiting the Grand Theater is basically my dream come.

t_ch: 你不就在这儿吗？
t_en: Aren't you here now?

t_ch: 哎呀，如果不是大剧院需要维修，我恐怕连走进那扇门都做不到。如此华贵的场所，和我完全是两个世界的造物。
t_en: Well, if it weren't for the fact that the Grand Theater needs renovating, I don't think I could even get through the door. An opulent place like this exists in an entirely different world from me.

t_ch: 这样宏伟的地方，里面居然也有我出的一份力，想想就觉得开心。
t_en: Just the thought of contributing to a place as magnificent as this makes me so happy.

t_ch: 只可惜，不能在这里一直待到正式演出。每次开演，我都会远远地站在外面看着。
t_en: It's just a shame that I can't stay here to see an official performance. I always stand somewhere outside and look at the theater every time a show is on.

t_ch: 有时我能听到一些声响，虽然不知道是哪儿发出的，但我想，那一定就是演奏的余音……
t_en: Sometimes, I can hear some sounds. I don't know where they are coming from, but I imagine that they must be the reverberations of the performance...

t_ch: 对了，你有看过歌剧院的演出吗？
t_en: Say, have you seen a show in the Grand Theater before?

t_ch: 暂时还没。
t_en: Not yet.

t_ch: 真可惜，我还说问问你的感受。
t_en: That's a shame. I was just about to ask your thoughts about the shows here.

t_ch: 看过几场。
t_en: I've seen a few.

t_ch: 真羡慕，你一定是位了不得的大人物。
t_en: Jealous! You must be someone very important.

t_ch: 我在这演过一场大戏。
t_en: I put on a big show here once.

t_ch: 哇塞！你原来是明星，真是太了不起了！
t_en: Whoa! So you are a star! That's incredible!

t_ch: 演出啊，会有些什么呢？是像梦泡电影那样？还是说有比流行曲目好听一百倍的歌？还是…总而言之，应该是我完全想象不出来的美梦吧。
t_en: What kind of show? Something like a dream bubble movie? Or some song that's a hundred times better than regular pop music? Or... Anyway, it must be some dream that I couldn't even begin to imagine.

t_ch: 隐夜鸫的工头说了，只要努力工作，总有一天我也能以宾客而非修理工的身份走进来。
t_en: The supervisor from Nightingale told me that if I worked hard, the day would come when I could be here as a guest and not someone doing construction.

t_ch: 一想到工友们纷纷向我投来敬佩和艳羡的目光，就连干活都比平时更有力气了。
t_en: When I think about the looks of admiration and envy my fellow workers would give me, I find myself feeling more upbeat about my work than usual.

t_ch: 要来聊聊吗？
t_en: Want to talk for a bit?

t_ch: 好呀，只是我这人没什么见识，希望不会让你无聊。
t_en: Okay, but I don't really know much about anything. I hope you won't get bored.

t_ch: 聊聊你的生活。
t_en: Let's talk about your life.

t_ch: 我住在公司提供的集体宿舍里，和几个室友挤在一起，但她们人都很好，每天我们都一起到「黎明的时刻」上班。
t_en: I live with a few people in a cramped dorm room provided by the IPC. They're all nice though. We go to the Moment of Daybreak for work every day.

t_ch: 入梦后，我们就会陆陆续续收到客户的单子，有时是单独给一些店铺做维修，有时是一起去填补大工程的空缺。
t_en: After we enter the Dreamscape, we'll receive orders from customers. Sometimes, it'll be a solo task to do maintenance on some shops. Other times, we'll need to work together to fill in the gap of a large project.

t_ch: 维修工作经常因为意外情况加班，所以我一般会订一份速食营养餐，去现实吃掉就立马回来。
t_en: Maintenance work often requires working overtime because something comes up, so I typically will order some kind of healthy fast food meal that I'll eat quickly in the real world, then rush back to work.

t_ch: 这么想来，我生活的地方其实是梦境，现实只是维持生命的餐厅而已。
t_en: Now that I think of it, I pretty much live my life in the Dreamscape. Reality is just a restaurant where I eat to keep my body alive.

t_ch: 不过这不也挺好吗？干的活越多，钱就越多，离梦想就越近。
t_en: But that's not bad, no? The more work I have, the more money I make, and the more money I make, the closer I am to achieving my dreams.

t_ch: 谈谈你的梦想。
t_en: Let's talk about your dream.

t_ch: 变成有钱的宾客！
t_en: I want to become a rich patron of this theater!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，很庸俗吧？可有钱真的能做到好多事，不是吗？
t_en: Hehe. Too basic, right? But really, there's so much you can do if you have money, you know?

t_ch: 把想去的地方全逛个遍，带爸妈到匹诺康尼最好的餐馆吃一顿，给室友每人带件礼物，买几束花放在床头，还有好多还没想到的。
t_en: Go to all the places I want, take my parents to the best restaurant in Penacony, give all my roommates presents, buy some flowers to put at my bedside, and so many other things I haven't thought of yet.

t_ch: 最后，像个上流人士一样走进大剧院，美美地享受一场演出。
t_en: Last but not least, I would be an upper-class person and waltz into the Grand Theater to enjoy a performance.

t_ch: 怎样，是不是你听着也开始羡慕了？
t_en: Just listening to me must have you feeling envious, right?

t_ch: 说说你的爱好。
t_en: Tell me about your hobbies.

t_ch: 在我还是学生的时候，很喜欢捏梦泡。
t_en: I used to love making dream bubbles when I was a student.

t_ch: 因为脑子里有许许多多的梦想都没法实现，所以只能自己做几个梦泡出来，满足一下那些异想天开的愿望。
t_en: I had all these dreams floating around in my head that I was never going to fulfill, so all I could do was make some dream bubbles to satisfy my unrealistic visions.

t_ch: 比如，我做过一艘超光速飞船，想要像电影里的驾驶员那样，享受在宇宙里高速航行的快乐。
t_en: Like how I had this dream of making a faster-than-light spaceship and being one of those pilots you see in the movies. I'd get to enjoy flying around the universe at blinding speeds.

t_ch: 可是，我只看过电影，飞船以外的细节完全做不出来。所以入梦之后，我就只能在船舱里孤零零地飘着，一直飘到梦醒。
t_en: But I had only seen the movies, so I couldn't think of any details other than the ship. Once I entered the dream, all I could do was float around by myself inside the ship until I woke up.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: 呃…原来宾客们都这么开放的吗？
t_en: Uh... Are all the guests this open-minded?

t_ch: 我…我个人在这方面还是保守派，所以…那个…对不起，不可以的！
t_en: I... I'm a bit conservative when it comes to that sort of thing. So... umm... sorry! No can do!

t_ch: 我先走了。
t_en: I'm heading off, then.

t_ch: 有机会再聊。
t_en: Let's talk again sometime.

t_ch: 真好，离开以后，你一定会遇上很多有趣的事情吧。
t_en: Great. You'll have all sorts of interesting things happen after you leave.

t_ch: 不过我会追上来的，勤奋是逐梦客成功的唯一道路，一直走下去，幸福总会到来。
t_en: But I'll catch up to you. Working hard is the only path to success for a Dreamchaser. Happiness will find you if you just keep going.

t_ch: 到时候，我要你也好好地崇拜我一下。
t_en: When the time comes, I want you to adore me.

t_ch: 欧利马笑呵呵地擦了把汗，疲惫的身体里，又被振奋地挤出几分活力。
t_en: Olma wiped off a bit of sweat as she giggled. The excitement allowed her tired body to squeeze out just a bit more energy.

t_ch: 名为梦想的神，一如往常地，用手掌捂住了她的双眼。
t_en: The goddess known as Dream covers her eyes with her hands as she always does.

t_ch: 省吃俭用，辛勤劳动，成功就在我的前方招手。
t_en: If I live frugally and work hard, success will be just around the corner.

t_ch: 知道吗？我的工资又涨了5％！平均下来，每天多到手的钱可以买三瓶苏乐达！
t_en: Did I tell you? My pay went up by 5%! That means with the extra money I get every day, I can basically buy three bottles of SoulGlad!

t_ch: 维修工这份活计虽然很累，但只要肯对自己狠下心来，比起许多看似体面的工作，收入要可观得多。而且我们没有团建！
t_en: Doing repair work is tough stuff, but if you are willing to be tough on yourself, the pay can be way better than jobs that seem like they'd be more respectable. On top of that, we don't have team-building activities!

t_ch: 很多人都喜欢做白日梦，如果自己出生在更富裕的家庭会怎样，可也就是想想而已。不够努力才是他们没钱的原因。
t_en: Lots of people like to daydream. They'll think about what life would be like if only they had been born into a richer family, but that's just empty thinking. The reason they don't have money is that they aren't working hard enough.

t_ch: 但我可以亲手改变自己的生活，只要踏实肯干，就像那些盛会巨星，名不见经传的小人物也可以成为闻名世界的大商人。
t_en: But I can change my life with my own two hands. I just need to be down-to-earth and work hard, just like the Festive Superstars. An unknown figure can become a world-famous businessperson.

t_ch: 哎，这么多钱，要怎么花才好呢。
t_en: Ah, this is a lot of money. How should I spend it?

t_ch: 乌云压顶般的阴霾骤然将欧利马的双瞳笼罩，她试图压抑身体的颤抖，但并不成功。
t_en: Olma's eyes cloud over with a sudden gloominess. She tries to suppress her body's trembling to no avail.

t_ch: 如果现在只有她一个人，你怀疑她甚至会直接瘫下来。
t_en: You suspect that if she were by herself, she would be falling to the floor.

t_ch: 未来该怎么办啊，现在这种日子，完全看不到头。
t_en: What am I going to do in the future? With how things are now, I can't see where it's all going.

t_ch: 唉…每天除了干活就是干活，可存款却不怎么见长。入职的时候，项目经理明明告诉我，只要多干几年，什么都会有的，可这都多少年了？
t_en: Ah... I just work day in and day out, but I'm not seeing my savings grow. When I first started my job, the boss told me anything could happen if I just worked harder for a few years. How many years have I been at it?

t_ch: 爸妈都说我这工作没有前途，可我还能去干什么？白日梦的前台也不招皮皮西呀。
t_en: My parents told me that my job has no career advancement prospects, but what else can I do? The front desk of The Reverie isn't going to hire a Pepeshi.

t_ch: 前段时间生病去医院走了一遭，好不容易屯的钱一下子又没了不少，天天这么干哪可能不生病，完全就是个无底洞。
t_en: I got sick a while back and went to the hospital. All the money I worked so hard to get went down the drain. How can I not get sick when I work this hard every day? It's a vicious cycle.

t_ch: 照现在这样子过下去，不吃不喝干上五百年，也赶不上人家开到剧院门口的一辆车。
t_en: If I keep going the way I have, even if I don't eat or drink for five hundred years, I still wouldn't be able to afford the kind of car people are driving up to the main door of the theater.

t_ch: 这日子过得就像在推一块石头，每次推上山，就会带着你滚回低谷，我真的快撑不下去了。
t_en: Each day feels like I'm pushing a massive boulder, and every time I reach the top of the mountain, it pushes me back down the valley. I really can't do this anymore.

t_ch: 欧利马的委屈涌上心头，愤怒的情绪重新泵入欧利马的血管，仿佛下一刻就会嘭地炸开。
t_en: Olma's grievances well up in her heart, and anger pumps into her blood vessels once again. She seems like she might explode.

t_ch: 多年来受的委屈，在骗局里持续的生活，这些东西止不住地在她的脑子里打转。
t_en: All of the wrongs she had endured over the years and the life she continued to lead in a scam, none of that could stop her mind from spinning.

t_ch: 我想通了…什么用辛苦创造财富，其实、其实都是假的！骗人的！
t_en: I've figured it out... All this talk of working hard to create wealth is actually nothing more than a fantasy! It's a scam!

t_ch: 设备损坏要自己花钱修，员工宿舍要租金，就连入梦池污染都要我们赔钱，你干脆让我把公司买下来得了。
t_en: If we break any equipment, we've got to pay for it ourselves. We've got to pay rent to live in the employee dorms. We've even got to pay compensation for pollution when entering the Dreampool. With all of that, you might as well let me buy the company.

t_ch: 那些高高在上的家伙有把我们当作人吗？我们只是齿轮和零件！损耗了就扔掉。工厂零件还要定期花钱保养呢。
t_en: Do the higher-ups even see us as people? We're nothing more than cogs and gears! We're easily replaceable. Even factory components require maintenance.

t_ch: 我猜，他们巴不得把我的心、我的肝和肺全都拿走，摆在橱窗里卖掉。
t_en: I suppose they can't wait to take my heart, my liver, and my lungs and put them on display to sell them.

t_ch: 然后，再把卖掉的钱拿走九分，将吃剩的残渣施舍给我，美其名曰赏赐。
t_en: And then, they'll run off with most of the money and give me the scraps that remain as a "reward."

t_ch: 这就是匹诺康尼所谓的美梦！
t_en: That's the Penaconian Dream for you!

t_ch: 真是越想越气，这苏乐达我是喝不下去了，送给你算了。
t_en: I feel furious just thinking about it. I can't keep drinking this SoulGlad. You take it.

t_ch: 设备损坏要自己花钱修，员工宿舍要租金，就连入梦池污染都要我们赔钱，你干脆让我把公司买下来得了。
t_en: If we break any equipment, we've got to pay for it ourselves. We've got to pay rent to live in the employee dorms. We've even got to pay compensation for pollution when entering the Dreampool. With all of that, you might as well let me buy the company.

t_ch: 那些高高在上的家伙有把我们当作人吗？我们只是齿轮和零件！损耗了就扔掉。工厂零件还要定期花钱保养呢。
t_en: Do the higher-ups even see us as people? We're nothing more than cogs and gears! We're easily replaceable. Even factory components require maintenance.

t_ch: 我猜，他们巴不得把我的心、我的肝和肺全都拿走，摆在橱窗里卖掉。
t_en: I suppose they can't wait to take my heart, my liver, and my lungs and put them on display to sell them.

t_ch: 然后，再把卖掉的钱拿走九分，将吃剩的残渣施舍给我，美其名曰赏赐——这就是匹诺康尼所谓的美梦！
t_en: And then, they'll run off with most of the money and give me the scraps that remain as a "reward." That's the Penaconian Dream!

t_ch: 欧利马叹了口气，不知道该露出什么表情，最终还是停留在一丝浅浅的微笑上。
t_en: Olma sighs. She seems uncertain about what expression to make and just holds the faintest of smiles.

t_ch: 此刻，你眼前的人似乎一下子老了几十岁，深沉而无奈。
t_en: Just at that moment, the person in front of you seems to have aged several decades. It's as if she were sinking deeper and helpless.

t_ch: 虽然生活的现状不佳，但，走一步算一步吧。
t_en: My life might not be great at the moment, but a step forward is still a step forward, I guess.

t_ch: 工作这事，无非也就是一种买卖，我出力，老板出钱。但老板有选择的权力，我没有，所以只能遵守他制定的交易规则。
t_en: Work is all a matter of buying and selling. I put in the effort; the boss puts in the money. The difference between us is that the boss has the right to choose, while I don't. All I can do is abide by the rules he sets for exchanging my work for money.

t_ch: 平心而论，以我的现状，想要成为那种能够自由进出大剧院的宾客，多半不现实。可整个匹诺康尼又有多少人是这样的宾客呢？
t_en: To be fair, becoming the kind of person who can freely enter the Grand Theater as a guest is pretty unfeasible based on my current circumstances. I wonder how many people in Penacony are able to walk right into this theater?

t_ch: 酒店前台，商店服务员，猎犬的警卫，其实大家的生活都是平凡普通的。盲目追求所谓的有钱，只会迷失自我，被老板画的饼骗到。
t_en: Attendants at hotels, clerks working in stores, Bloodhound guards, all of us lead average lives. Blindly chasing after the goal of being wealthy will just cause us to lose ourselves. We'll be duped by the pie our bosses draw for us.

t_ch: 我现在意识到，比起一味的攒钱，适当地做一些改善生活的事情要重要得多。
t_en: I've come to realize that doing things to improve our lives is more important than blindly making money.

t_ch: 多关心关心自己的身体健康，抽空回去看看爸妈，到公园里去躺着发会儿呆，再去海边弄点薯条。听起来不错吧？
t_en: Things like being mindful of your health, taking time to see your parents, stretching out at a park to space out for a while, or enjoying some fries on the beach. That all sounds pretty good, right?

t_ch: 我开始怀念昨日品尝到的佳酿了——冽如清溪、甘如花蜜，就好似流动的黄金、凝固的日色……
t_en: I'm starting to miss the fragrant brew I tasted yesterday — cool as a fresh stream, and that honeyed liquid was akin to molten gold or the color of the sun itself...

t_ch: 这乏味的工作属实枯燥，今天该怎么打发时间呢…嗯？
t_en: This mundane job is too boring. How should I spend my time today... Eh?

t_ch: 瞧瞧，这鬼地方竟来了位如此可爱的客人…有什么我能为你做的吗，亲爱的？
t_en: Oh look, such a lovely-looking customer in such a forsaken place... What can I do for you, darling?

t_ch: 你在这做什么？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 合理利用工作时间，调整个人办公状态…直白点说，就是忙里偷闲、无聊消遣。
t_en: To astutely utilize my work hours and organize my own work attitude... Or more bluntly, to find time to slack off at work and relieve my boredom, darling.

t_ch: 谁是你亲爱的。
t_en: Ew, who's your darling?

t_ch: 哦~我无意冒犯。很抱歉，我惯常如此，或许你可以将其视作某种表达友好的方式。
t_en: Ooh, I didn't mean to offend. I apologize. It's just a force of habit. You can just take it as my way of a friendly greeting.

t_ch: 你说话的方式有点瘆人…
t_en: The way you talk is a bit creepy...

t_ch: 哈哈…抱歉，比起建筑，我更崇尚诗词之美，言语也难免受其影响，请你见谅。
t_en: Haha... I apologize. I much prefer to revel in the beauty of poetry than architecture, and my speech may have been affected as well. Please forgive me.

t_ch: 那么，请容我贸然询问，你前来此地，是否怀有某种目的……
t_en: Now then, if you don't mind my asking: Do you perhaps have a motive for coming to this place...

t_ch: 别担心，我无意打探你的秘密，只是假如你此刻并不匆忙，请让我占用些许时光，为你带来一份乐事——
t_en: Don't worry, I do not mean to pry into your secrets. It's just that — if you're not too busy — I would ask that you lend me some of your time to bring you an item of pleasure...

t_ch: 瞧！这是我根据球笼竞速发明的改良型飞行棋，规则简单易懂……
t_en: Look! This is a modified version of Aero-Chess I invented based off the Spheroid Races. The rules are easy to understand...

t_ch: 男人用某种令人无法拒绝的语气介绍着他的小游戏，你出于好奇，决定尝试一下……
t_en: In a tone that prevents one from refusing his request, the man introduces his fun little game. Out of sheer curiosity, you decide to try it...

t_ch: 一局结束，你意犹未尽地抬起头，发现竟已过去了三个系统时。
t_en: You finish a round, and when you raise your head, you are flabbergasted: Three system hours have passed.

t_ch: 真是位可敬的对手——托你的福，亲爱的，我度过了非常有意义的一天。
t_en: You are truly a worthy opponent. My day has been meaningfully spent thanks to you, darling.

t_ch: 虽然我也想继续与你在棋盘上角逐厮杀，但非常可惜，下班时间已经到了。
t_en: I would like to continue our intense clashes on the board, but alas, it is time for me to punch the clock.

t_ch: 但请别沮丧，我最近会时常流连于剧院，直到那些不可爱的「瑕疵」被修补完毕。
t_en: But do not despair — I will frequent the theater these days, till those unlovely "flaws" are fixed.

t_ch: 倘若你仍想继续我们的战局，明日我依旧恭候于此。
t_en: If you wish to continue our battle, I will be waiting for you here tomorrow.

t_ch: 又或者…倘若你企盼新鲜的乐趣，我也可以为你献上更多惊喜。
t_en: Or perhaps... if you wish for more refreshingly fun times, I have more surprises in store for you.

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 当然可以，亲爱的。如果是你的话，我将很乐意献上宝贵的下班时间。
t_en: Of course you can, darling. I'd be more than willing to spend my precious off-hours with you.

t_ch: 你是什么人？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我啊…我是隐夜鸫群中的无名小鸟，一位无足轻重的仆从、家族肆意驱使的家佣。
t_en: Me...? I'm just a nobody in the Nightingale Family. An insignificant servant, an attendant bound to the every whim of The Family.

t_ch: 我受命而来，打扫老爷夫人们打碎的花瓶、修理少爷小姐们弄坏的玩具。
t_en: I was instructed to come here and clean up the vases that the masters and mistresses broke, and to fix the toys damaged by the young masters and misses.

t_ch: 瞧瞧这娃娃房、瞧瞧这大剧院，被人搞得破破烂烂——就像是有人在此同星神打了一场！
t_en: Just look at this child's room, and this giant theater — it's been ruined. It's as if someone had an epic battle with an Aeon in here!

t_ch: 这么消极怠工没问题吗？
t_en: You sure it's all right to be so indolent with your work?

t_ch: 多谢你的关心，亲爱的。但那些老爷们向来只看结果，不关心过程。
t_en: Thank you for your concern, darling, but all those old geezers care about are results, and not how they are met.

t_ch: 要是他们哪天突发奇想，亲自跑来监工，那可就再好不过啦。我可是巴不得有人来接手这烂摊子……
t_en: If they suddenly decided to show up on a whim to check in on my progress, that would be even more wonderful. I can't wait to palm this horrid job off to someone else...

t_ch: 当然——前提是他们当真能找到合适的人来接手。
t_en: Of course, that is if they could ever find someone more accomplished to handle this job.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 你要走了吗，亲爱的？真可惜，我还想多听听你那美妙的嗓音呢。
t_en: Leaving so soon, darling? What a pity, I was hoping to enjoy your mesmerizing voice a bit longer.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 这可真是份甜蜜的叨扰，期待再次与你相见。
t_en: This chat was truly a sweet distraction. I do hope to see you again.

t_ch: 能再次见到你我很开心，亲爱的。
t_en: It's so nice to see you again, darling.

t_ch: 你的到来令我耳目皆新，能从这乏味单调的工作中寻得一丝慰藉。
t_en: Your mere presence has bequeathed me a sliver of comfort from the tedium of this job.

t_ch: 自从我那同样热爱诗歌的挚友不知所踪，我已许久未曾有过如此感受…说来我是否还未向你介绍过我的挚友——
t_en: Ever since my kindred friend — an avid compatriot of poetry like me — disappeared, I have not felt this way for a long time... Speaking of which, I haven't introduced that bosom friend of mine...

t_ch: 我们总是形影不离、总是共同在诗歌与雕塑的美中遨游；我们无所不谈，契合如同灵魂的侣伴，直到……
t_en: We were thick as thieves, meandering through the splendor of poetry and sculptures together. There was nothing we would not talk about, as if our spirits were in perfect synch, until...

t_ch: 我虽尚无法知晓「死亡」的真相究竟如何，但总有一天，我们终会回到彼此身边。
t_en: I may not know the truth behind "Death," but one day, we will return to each other's sides.

t_ch: 不过在那之前…要再来一局飞行棋吗？还是来尝试一下我最近研究出来的惊梦纸牌？
t_en: But before that... How about another round of Aero-Chess? Or would you like to have a go at my newly-discovered Dreamjolt Cards?

t_ch: 我倒是觉得，这梦境并无修缮的必要……
t_en: I rather think that this Dreamscape does not require any fixing...

t_ch: 我虽受命修缮此地，心中不免暗生惫怠。美梦往往令人乏味，混沌反而流光溢彩。
t_en: I may have been instructed to fix up this place, but tiresome darkness fills my heart. Dreams are often mundane, and chaos is just so much more colorful.

t_ch: 庭中蔷薇美则美矣，却不免怯懦、娇娇滴滴…倒不如教自由的继续自由，支离的愈发支离。
t_en: The roses in the yard are so gorgeous, yet so timid, so delicate and helpless... We might as well teach the free to continue to be free, and the splintered to become more fractured.

t_ch: （听他讲完）
t_en: (Let him finish)

t_ch: 筑梦者当如诗人与英雄，将梦境化作不屈的意志、斗争的武器，而非多愁善感的欢歌、无病呻吟的愁绪。
t_en: Dreambuilders are like poets and heroes, turning Dreamscapes into weapons of combat with indomitable wills — not odes of sentimental joy, nor melancholic moans of obscurantism.

t_ch: 若有一天，我与世界作别，请勿陈鲜花于棺柩前，请为我呈上桂冠、甲胄与宝剑……
t_en: If the day would come when I would bid farewell to this world, do not offer fresh blooms before my casket, but a laurel crown, a chest of armor, and a sharp sword...

t_ch: 说人话。
t_en: Speak human please.

t_ch: 唉，看来你也是没有艺术灵光的腐朽之人——我的意思是工作太无聊了，丝毫没有浪漫的情调！
t_en: *sigh* It appears that you are also not one touched by the enlightenment of art. I meant that work is so boring, and devoid of any romantic ambience!

t_ch: 谁又能想到，美梦竟如此令人失望……
t_en: But who knew that Dreamscapes would be such a disappointment...

t_ch: 蠹虫…梦里梦外，无处不在——那是滔天洪水无法濯净的污浊，遍野烈焰无法燃尽的腐败。
t_en: Vermin... They're anywhere and everywhere, within and without dreams — it is a rot that cannot be cleansed by deluges, a corruption that cannot be extinguished by rampant wildfire.

t_ch: 我曾受邀为暴君筑碑、佞臣塑像，故得见如何人命草芥、荒谬荒唐。他们许我以万千金纸、无限荣光，而我自然当倾情以报、答此重赏——
t_en: I have been invited to erect monuments in honor of tyrants and sculpt statues for sycophants — I have witnessed both the absurdity of the world and the insignificance of human lives. They have bestowed me with countless riches and infinite glory, and naturally, I am honor-bound to return the favor and respond to these hefty rewards...

t_ch: 我以鸽血石为腐肉、羊脂玉作骸骨，以黄金铸魔鬼、白银造妖巫；腐黑曜为坏疽，败翡翠作流毒…这亲手打造的华庭，我将其命名为「审判的幻景」。
t_en: I used pigeon blood rubies as carrion, suet jade as bones, forged demons in gold, and created wights in silver. Putrid obsidian as gangrene, flawed jade as poison... This court built with my own hands, I will name it the "Illusion of Judgment."

t_ch: （听他讲完）
t_en: (Let him finish)

t_ch: 而我为此付出了什么呢？不过是些微不足道的代价：他们谓我恶魔，将我流放，咒我颠沛流离，令我不得归乡。
t_en: And what have I paid for with this? A meager price not worth mentioning: They labeled me a devil, exiled me, cursed me with vagrancy, and forced me to never return to my hometown.

t_ch: 但彼时我心中尚存希望，相信世间必有自由的美梦、无垢的圣堂……
t_en: But now, hope sparks in my heart. I believe the world must have dreams of freedom, an untainted sanctuary...

t_ch: …自由的美梦…哈哈，何其荒唐！
t_en: ...Dreams of freedom... Haha, how absurd!

t_ch: 说人话。
t_en: Speak human please.

t_ch: 你…我是说我渴望着一片能够自由创造艺术的天地，但那并不存在于星海，也不会存在于梦中！
t_en: You... I meant that I yearn for a haven where I can freely create art, but it won't exist in the stars, nor does it in dreams!

t_ch: 这里全都是庸俗的家伙——恕我冒昧，你也一样！
t_en: These are all riffraff here — pardon me if I may say so, but so are you!

t_ch: 男人的目光落在虚空，恬淡笑意中带着某种近乎神圣的喜悦。
t_en: The man's gaze falls upon the void, his indifferent smile radiating a certain, almost divine joy.

t_ch: 亲爱的，你可曾听说过「欧福里翁」？
t_en: Darling, have you heard of Euphorion?

t_ch: 那是颗崇高的星球，是自由的殿堂、是理想的黄金乡——
t_en: That is a sublime planet, a sanctum of freedom, a utopia of ideals...

t_ch: 那里的人们兼具智慧、美丽与勇气，他们生来典雅，因永不熄灭的宏愿而伟大。
t_en: The people there are erudite, beautiful, and brave. They are born elegant, and great in their inextinguishable ambition of grandeur.

t_ch: 那里不存在悲愁、不存在腐朽，那里繁星汇集、钟灵毓秀……
t_en: Neither sorrow nor decadence exist there. Myriad stars gather there, where talents and their brethren congregate...

t_ch: （听他讲完）
t_en: (Let him finish)

t_ch: 只可惜…那星球已沉入虚无之海，足足一个琥珀纪之久，自此再无人有幸抵达那乌托邦的港口。
t_en: Alas... That planet is submerged in the boundless sea of Nihility — for an entire Amber Era, none have had the fortune of approaching this utopian harbor.

t_ch: 但我心中尚存希望…或许终有一日，我将泅渡那方混沌、潜入那片虚无，寻得那永恒的乐土……
t_en: But hope persists in my heart... Perhaps one day, I will traverse that chaos, dive into that void, and find that eternal paradise...

t_ch: 说人话。
t_en: Speak human please.

t_ch: 呵呵，我是想说——我总有一天会找到那里，哪怕永远被困在虚无的黑暗，也比困在盲目庸俗的世界中要好。
t_en: Heh-heh, what I meant was that I will one day find that place. Even if I am eternally trapped in that dark void, it is still better than being trapped in this blind, uncouth world.

t_ch: 准备好了吗，朋友们？那么，布莱茨，从你开始……
t_en: Everybody ready? Alright, Blite, you're up first...

t_ch: 嗯，不错、不错…就是这样，太美妙了！…哦，不，不不不！施托赫森，你在做什么？！这里不该是渐强的！……
t_en: Hmm, not bad, not bad... That's it, then. Wonderful! ... Er, no no no! Storsen, what are you doing? It's not time for the crescendo yet!

t_ch: 天啊，洛莲！你又为什么要发出那种声音？请注意我的手势！…哎，算了算了，今天就到这里吧！我们改日再排练。
t_en: Ack, Lorraine! Why are you making that noise again? Please, pay attention to my conducting! ... *sigh* Enough, forget about it. That's all for today! We'll rehearse again another day.

t_ch: 这位高贵的指挥家似乎正在自言自语…看起来可真古怪。
t_en: This haughty conductor seems to be talking to himself... What a weirdo.

t_ch: 你在自言自语什么呢？
t_en: Are you talking to yourself?

t_ch: …自言自语？不不不，我只是在…和家人们排练。
t_en: ...Talking to myself? No no no, I was just... rehearsing with my family members.

t_ch: 一个人也能排练吗？
t_en: Can one person rehearse alone?

t_ch: 一个人？哈哈，不不不…我只是在和家人们排练。
t_en: Alone? Haha, no no no... I was rehearsing together with my family members.

t_ch: 没想到大剧院里还能听脱口秀。
t_en: I didn't expect to find stand-up comedy in the Grand Theater.

t_ch: 脱口秀？！不，这可是为了「谐乐大典」而生的谐乐，不是那种，呃…不太严肃的艺术表现形式。
t_en: Stand-up comedy? No, this is just a piece for the Charmony Festival. Not like that, er... less serious form of art.

t_ch: 你呢，朋友？据我所知，现在的大剧院可不是寻常游客能随意出入的地方。
t_en: And you, friend? As I understand it, the Grand Theater doesn't just let in any ordinary tourists for a visit these days.

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}？难道是星穹列车的那位……
t_en: {NICKNAME}? Don't tell me you're the one with the Astral Express...

t_ch: 我是星穹列车的成员。
t_en: I'm part of the Astral Express's crew.

t_ch: 啊，星穹列车……
t_en: Ah, the Astral Express...

t_ch: 我是浣熊。
t_en: I'm a raccoon.

t_ch: 你是…什么？浣熊？
t_en: You're a... what? Raccoon?

t_ch: 哦，哦！我知道了，你是说《银河球棒侠传说》的主人公吧。看你这身打扮……
t_en: Oh! I get it. You mean the main hero from the Legend of the Galactic Baseballer. Judging by how you're all dressed up...

t_ch: …失敬失敬。各位的事迹我听说了，感谢你们让我离谐乐大典的舞台更进一步。
t_en: ...Excuse me. I've heard about your deeds. Thank you for bringing me one step closer to center stage at the Charmony Festival.

t_ch: 我可以请教一些问题吗？
t_en: May I please ask you a few questions?

t_ch: 当然！各位可是我的大恩人哪。
t_en: Certainly! You are all benefactors of mine.

t_ch: 我怎么就成你恩人了？
t_en: When exactly did I become your benefactor?

t_ch: 哈哈，这当然是因为你们搞定了「谐乐大典」原定的调弦师啦。
t_en: Haha, of course it's when you sorted out the original Chordmaster for the Charmony Festival.

t_ch: 至于发生了什么，想必你比我更清楚。鸢尾花家系现在正在重新选拔调弦师…虽然他们都觉得知更鸟小姐还会重返舞台，但我不这么认为。
t_en: You know better than I do as to what happened. The Iris Family is now auditioning for another Chordmaster... and despite them all thinking Miss Robin is ready to make a return to the stage, I beg to differ.

t_ch: 要登上「谐乐大典」舞台的只可能是我——我已经梦见神主不止一次了，祂说我迟早会成为调弦师，成为众望所归……
t_en: The only one who can mount the stage at the Charmony Festival is me — the Great One has visited me in my dreams more than once to tell me that sooner or later I am going to be a Chordmaster beloved by the masses...

t_ch: …现在是时候了——我再也不用私下去琴房兼职调弦了——经此一役，我即将走上人生巅峰！
t_en: ...And now it's time — I won't have to moonlight in secret anymore at the piano shop. After this, I'm going to climb to the pinnacle of success!

t_ch: 想请你介绍一下「谐乐大典」。
t_en: Can you tell me about the Charmony Festival?

t_ch: 「谐乐大典」？你就简单理解为我们的一种「祈愿」仪式吧。
t_en: The Charmony Festival? You can just think of it as a kind of "prayer" ceremony for us.

t_ch: 家族在银河各地的分家都会不定期举行类似的仪式，规模也各不相同。据我所知，匹诺康尼的谐乐大典应该是各大分家里最隆重的，因此一纪里只会举办一次。
t_en: The Family holds similar ceremonies of differing sizes every now and then across the various branches spread throughout the Cosmos. As far as I know, Penacony's Charmony Festival is the most prestigious of The Family's, and so it's only staged once every Amber Era.

t_ch: 在谐乐大典上，我们为同一种梦想祈愿，对同一种愿景祷告。以理想凝聚集群，「众赞之弦」便会降临，响应我们的愿望，赐给我们无上的极乐……
t_en: During the Charmony Festival, we pray for a common dream and for a common vision. Brought together under the same ideals, the "Harmony of Praise" will arrive, answering our prayers and granting supreme bliss...

t_ch: …当然，这只是通常情况。如果是「无限夫长」阿伊里涅夫响应召唤，那有人可要吃苦头了。
t_en: ...Of course, that's just how it usually happens. If it's Aelenev, the Eternal Centurion, who answers the call, then someone is going to get punished.

t_ch: 他只会出现在家族守护家人的战争中，将众家人团结为一支同调的军队。被他消灭的人也会被同化，成为我们的一员——从某种角度来说，也算是好处吧，哈哈。
t_en: He only shows up for The Family's wars, uniting them into a cohesive force — and everyone he destroys ends up getting assimilated, becoming one of us. In many ways, it's a good thing, haha.

t_ch: 你的幻想家人是怎么回事？
t_en: What's the deal with your little imaginary family members, then?

t_ch: 幻想家人？这种说法可有点冒犯了：布莱茨、施托赫森、洛莲他们只是自愿放弃了肉身而已——他们可是「托斯卡尼乐团」不可或缺的家人！
t_en: Imaginary family? That's offensive to Blite, Storsen, and Lorraine, who all volunteered to give up their bodies — they're all indispensable family members of the Toskani Orchestra!

t_ch: 自愿放弃肉身？
t_en: They volunteered to give up their own bodies?

t_ch: 嗐，表情别太难看，朋友，事情不是你想象的那样。
t_en: Hey, don't look so distraught. It's not as bad as you think.

t_ch: 「自愿放弃肉身」可是第Ⅶ音级的家人们梦寐以求的*特权*。他们初入家族，虽然能感受到其他家人的情绪和集群之声的模糊指引，但也只是浅尝辄止。
t_en: "Voluntary body abandonment" is a special privilege that scale degree VII Family members can only dream of. When they first enter The Family, they can sense the emotions and hear the vague guidance of other Family members, but it's all just superficial.

t_ch: 跨越了最初的{RUBY_B#「情绪的同谐」}第Ⅶ音级{RUBY_E#}，我们才有机会与集群之声融为一体…而且可不是说融合就融合——只有通过选拔的家人才能享受这种福利。
t_en: Only after the first {RUBY_B#"Emotional Harmony"}scale degree VII{RUBY_E#} do we get the chance to merge with the other Family voices... and not just anyone gets to merge — only those selected Family members are able to enjoy this privilege.

t_ch: 所以，你懂吗？我的意思是…虽然神主谱写的每个音符都同样重要，但有的音符比其他音符更重要…我是说，更*同谐*，哈哈。
t_en: So then, do you get it? What I mean is... despite every note being created with equal importance by the Great One, in actual fact some notes are more important than others... I'm saying, some of them are more Harmonious, haha.

t_ch: 「托斯卡尼乐团」？
t_en: Toskani Orchestra?

t_ch: 没错，托斯卡尼就是我——我是这个乐团的总指挥。
t_en: That's right. I'm Toskani — and I'm the conductor of this orchestra.

t_ch: 我们乐团总共有<unbreak>72</unbreak>位成员，算上我。布莱茨是我们的第一小提琴，施托赫森是首席双簧管，洛莲负责竖琴。
t_en: We have <unbreak>72</unbreak> members in our orchestra, including me. Blite sits as our first violin, Storsen is our first oboe, and Lorraine is our harpist.

t_ch: 我们基本都是处于{RUBY_B#「言语的同谐」}第Ⅵ音级{RUBY_E#}的家人，能够与其他成员通过「共鸣」来传递信息，清晰理解集群之声呼唤。而为了更好地团结在一起…我们合而为一了。
t_en: We are basically Family members of {RUBY_B#"Verbal Harmony"}scale degree VI{RUBY_E#} who can communicate with each other using "resonance" to transfer information and understand clearly the calls of The Family voices. And to show better solidarity with one another... we decided to unite as one.

t_ch: 好吧，我不深究了。
t_en: Alright. That's enough for me.

t_ch: 噢，别客气！为外人宣扬家族的谐乐是我的使命，你可以尽情提问。
t_en: Oh, you're welcome! Promoting The Family's harmony to others is my mission, so ask away about anything you'd like.

t_ch: 先到这里吧。
t_en: Let's end the chat here, then.

t_ch: 噢，就这样了？好吧，但如果你还有疑问，我随时可以效劳。
t_en: Oh, is that so? Fine, but if you have any other questions, I'm available any time to help.

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 再见，恩人！我也要专心准备…呃，「谐乐大典」了。
t_en: Goodbye, o patron! I have to focus on getting ready too for... er, the Charmony Festival.

t_ch: 托斯卡尼对着空气念念有词，满脸都是断定自己即将走上人生巅峰的喜悦。
t_en: Toskani mutters a few words to himself, ecstatic in the belief that he is about to reach the zenith of his life.

t_ch: 天助我也，天助我也！都说星穹列车热心肠，这回总算是让我见识到了！
t_en: The Great One blesses me! Everyone knows how kind-hearted the crew is, and now I get to witness it myself.

t_ch: 朋友们，你们也很开心吧？我们马上就能登上「谐乐大典」的舞台啦！
t_en: Friends, are you all happy too? We'll soon be center-stage at the Charmony Festival!

t_ch: 嗨，我们又见面了，托斯卡尼乐团的大救星！我们正在加紧排练呢——选拔在即，可不能辜负了你们的好意！
t_en: Hey, it's the savior of the Toskani Orchestra! We meet again. We're knee-deep in rehearsals — auditions are coming up, and there's no way we're going to let you down!

t_ch: 你知道吗？如果不是你们出手干预谐乐大典，像我们这种只有{RUBY_B#「言语的同谐」}第Ⅵ音级{RUBY_E#}的家人根本没可能登上剧院舞台。
t_en: Do you know that if you hadn't stepped in to help with the Charmony Festival, Family members like us with only {RUBY_B#"Verbal Harmony"}scale degree VI{RUBY_E#} would have no hope of making it on stage?

t_ch: 我和乐团商量了一下，觉得无论如何都得报答各位。可惜我们只是个不入流的小乐团，没法准备太昂贵的礼品……
t_en: I've chatted this over with the orchestra, and feel like we have to repay you no matter what happens. But unfortunately, we're only a small group of amateurs, so we couldn't get you anything expensive...

t_ch: 这、这是我最喜欢的匹诺康尼小吃，一周才能吃上一次的那种——一点心意，还请你笑纳！
t_en: This here is my favorite local snack here in Penacony, the kind of treat I only splash out for once a week — just a little token of thanks for you!

t_ch: 嗨，我们又见面了，托斯卡尼乐团的大救星！如果不是你们出手干预谐乐大典，像我们这种只有{RUBY_B#「言语的同谐」}第Ⅵ音级{RUBY_E#}的家人根本没可能登上剧院舞台。
t_en: Hey, it's the savior of the Toskani Orchestra! We meet again. Do you know that if you hadn't stepped in to help with the Charmony Festival, Family members like us with only {RUBY_B#"Verbal Harmony"} scale degree VI{RUBY_E#} would have no hope of making it on stage?

t_ch: 我们正在加紧排练呢——选拔在即，可不能辜负了你们的好意！
t_en: We're knee-deep in rehearsals — auditions are coming up, and there's no way we're going to let you down!

t_ch: 朋友们，我们要不还是别白费力气了……
t_en: Friends, how about we stop wasting our energy...

t_ch: 皮皮西指挥家看起来有些落寞。
t_en: The Pepeshi conductor looked a little defeated.

t_ch: 哎…虽然说这次「谐乐大典」出了大乱子，可这么隆重的典礼，哪里轮得到我们这种第Ⅵ音级的小虾米露面？
t_en: *sigh*... Despite all the mess with this year's Charmony Festival, such a grand ceremony as that should make no space for small fries like us at scale degree VI, right?

t_ch: 知更鸟小姐，那位银河巨星，你认识吧？听说她是第Ⅴ音级的家族成员…不过只比我们高一级，但她和我们已经完全是两个世界的人了。
t_en: Miss Robin, do you know that cosmic superstar over there? I heard she's a scale degree V Family member... that may only be one step above us, but she may as well have come from a completely different world.

t_ch: 五线谱上，第一线到第五线充其量也只有一根指头的距离。但落到现实里，哪怕再让我们多活几辈子，那也弥补不了音符与音符之间的差距。
t_en: On a musical stave, the distance between the first and the fifth lines is no more than a finger's width at most. But in real life — even if we lived out a few more lives — it still wouldn't be enough to contain the gaps between the notes.

t_ch: 说老实话，我们乐团里的大部分人都有点泄气了…可出路又在哪里？我们也说不清。
t_en: To be honest, most of us in the orchestra have started to lose faith... But how can we pivot? We aren't sure.

t_ch: 如果还是走老路，搞那些传统谐乐，我们是绝对出不了头的！
t_en: If we continue down the old path and keep doing all that traditional music, we'll never be able to make it!

t_ch: 给我燥起来！——<size=28>「我们要用生命燃烧下去，点燃孤独的白矮星！」</size>
t_en: Let's mosh! — <size=28>"We'll burn with our life, 'cause the white star needs a spark to ignite!"</size>

t_ch: 听我说，朋友们！还记得我们的梦想吗？我们要在「谐乐大典」的舞台上留下让世人永远传唱的旋律！
t_en: Listen up, friends! Remember our dream? We're going to rock a melody on the Charmony Festival stage that'll make the whole world sing!

t_ch: 我们不能继续走老路了！机会只有一次，我们必须唱出风格，唱出水平，给鸢尾花家系那帮*古典名家*一点小小的现代震撼！
t_en: We can't keep going on like this! You only get one shot, and we have to use our style and our talent to rock that fusty old classical Iris Family a rockin' good shock!

t_ch: 想想爱墨瑞得-Ⅲ，想想咱们的主推！想想大卫，想想戴夫，想想席德，想想博纳姆！
t_en: Think of Emerald-III, and think of all our top picks! Think of David, think of Daeve, think of Sid, and think of Bonham!

t_ch: 他们面对红巨星的时候怂了吗？没有，那咱们也不能怂！鸢尾花的评委毁灭不了一整个星系…他们连根毛都算不上！
t_en: Were they scared when they faced down the superstar? No — so neither should we! The Iris Family judges can't destroy an entire star system... They're small fry.

t_ch: 让他们看看新时代*交响摇滚*的厉害，让他们好好感受感受《廉价救赎》的含金量！都给我摇起来，燥起来，跟我一起唱——
t_en: Let's show them the power of modern orchestral rock and prove the worth of Cheap Salvation! Let's get rock and rollin' — everybody sing along with me now...

t_ch: <size=28>「懦夫只会向外逃去，口中却嚷着救赎之名——喔哦哦哦！」</size>
t_en: <size=28>"Only cowards flee while shouting the name of the redeemer — oh-yeah-yeah!"</size>

t_ch: 我们不能继续走老路了！机会只有一次，我们必须唱出风格，唱出水平，给鸢尾花家系那帮*古典名家*一点小小的现代震撼！
t_en: We can't keep going on like this! You only get one shot, and we have to use our style and our talent to rock that fusty old classical Iris Family a rockin' good shock!

t_ch: 想想爱墨瑞得-Ⅲ，想想咱们的主推！想想大卫，想想戴夫，想想席德，想想博纳姆！
t_en: Think of Emerald-III, and think of all our top picks! Think of David, think of Daeve, think of Sid, and think of Bonham!

t_ch: 他们面对红巨星的时候怂了吗？没有，那咱们也不能怂！鸢尾花的评委毁灭不了一整个星系…他们连根毛都算不上！
t_en: Were they scared when they faced down the superstar? No — so neither should we! The Iris Family judges can't destroy an entire star system... They're small fry.

t_ch: 让他们看看新时代*交响摇滚*的厉害，让他们好好感受感受《廉价救赎》的含金量！都给我摇起来，燥起来！
t_en: Let's show them the power of modern orchestral rock and prove the worth of Cheap Salvation! Let's get rock and rollin'!

t_ch: 托斯卡尼乐团现在正在中场休息。你想聊聊吗？
t_en: The Toskani Orchestra is taking a breather right now. Do you want to have a chat?

t_ch: 嗯…这次排练效果不错。家人们，记住这种感觉，把它带到舞台上。
t_en: Mmm... well, today's rehearsal went great. Remember this feeling, my Family, and bring it with you onto the stage.

t_ch: 现在先休息一下吧。哦，对了，布莱茨，记得检查下你的琴——最后一句稍微有点跑调。
t_en: Now let's take a breather. Oh yeah — Blite, remember to get your piano checked. That last line was a little out of tune.

t_ch: 没事，问题不大，观众们听不出来…但你是艺术家，别辜负了你的琴和作品。
t_en: It's cool. No big deal. The audience won't be able to tell... But you're an artist, so you shouldn't be letting down your instrument and your work.

t_ch: 看看这个世界，简直是无聊透顶——
t_en: Just look at this world. It's so boring, through and through...

t_ch: 我无意针对任何人，但必须得说，会场里进行的那些挑战都极其愚蠢，毫无技巧可言。
t_en: I don't mean to criticize, but I have to say that all the challenges were so stupid that they barely took any skill.

t_ch: 什么「演技派」舞台，什么「枪火」的试炼，不过是用来填充流程的乏味环节，连打发时间都显得枯燥。
t_en: The "School of Acting" stage, the "Gunfire" trial... they're just boring fillers used to speed things up and make even killing time seem boring.

t_ch: 就拿我的故乡——朋克洛德来举例，那里曾举办过一场声势浩大的线下寻宝活动，完美融合了冒险游戏和现实谜题，可以称得上是完美的设计。
t_en: Take my homeworld for example: Punklorde. We once put on an enormous offline treasure hunt that perfectly integrated adventure gaming with real-life puzzles. The design was perfect.

t_ch: 你知道那次夺宝传奇的胜出者是谁吗？
t_en: Do you know who the winner of the great treasure hunt that day was?

t_ch: 谁问你了？
t_en: Who asked you?

t_ch: 呃。没关系，从你脸上的表情看得出来，你肯定很想知道。
t_en: Er, fine. That's okay. I can tell just by the look on your face that you'd love to know.

t_ch: 所以是谁？
t_en: So, who was it?

t_ch: 既然你如此好奇，就告诉你吧。实不相瞒，正是本人，朋克洛德的超级骇客——「石剑」！
t_en: Seeing as you're so curious, I'll tell you who. The truth is, it was none other than me — the super hacker of Punklorde — Stoneblade!

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 想说什么就赶快，超级骇客的时间比你想的还要值钱。
t_en: If you've got something to say, make it snappy. A hacker's time is worth far more than you think.

t_ch: 哦。
t_en: Oh.

t_ch: 哼，好心提醒你，小灰毛，太过轻视眼前的人可是会吃苦头的。
t_en: Hmph, may I please remind you, Gray Hair, that you shouldn't insult people, or you might regret it.

t_ch: 整个匹诺康尼的通信网络都在我的掌控之中，想篡改你账户里的数据对我来说轻而易举——我的权能超乎你的想象，别逼我出手。
t_en: I have all of Penacony's communication network in the palm of my hand, and changing your account data is a piece of cake for someone like me — my powers are far greater than you could ever imagine. Don't force my hand.

t_ch: 请。
t_en: Please.

t_ch: 不知好歹的家伙，必须给你点教训瞧瞧。
t_en: You just don't know what's best for you. Let's see how you like me teaching you a little lesson.

t_ch: 「石剑」伸手在你的眼前虚晃了下，忽然你的视野闪过了几缕华丽光彩。
t_en: Stoneblade lifts his hand up and does a flick. Suddenly, a glorious brilliance flashes before your eyes.

t_ch: 回去好好检查你的账户吧，别以为我站在这什么都没做，你就能平安无事。超级骇客的手段总是出乎普通人的想象。
t_en: Go back and check over your account. Don't think that you're safe and sound just because I'm standing here doing nothing. Most super hacks are usually beyond the comprehension of normies.

t_ch: 就算你没发现任何变化，你的数据中也已被埋下病毒，以后睡觉别睡太死。
t_en: Even if you don't notice any changes, your data's already been corrupted with a virus, so don't sleep too soundly in the future.

t_ch: 只能说，懂的都懂，不懂的——也没必要过多解释。
t_en: All I can say is, if you get it, you get it. There's nothing more to explain.

t_ch: 典。
t_en: No shinola.

t_ch: 啥？你说什么？敢不敢再说一遍？！我超级骇客还从来没怕过谁！
t_en: Huh? What did you say? Just you try saying that again! I've never been afraid of anyone before in my life!

t_ch: 急了？
t_en: You mad?

t_ch: …你！必须让你长长记性！
t_en: ...Why, you! I'll make sure you never forget!

t_ch: 「石剑」伸手在你的眼前虚晃了下，忽然你的视野闪过了几缕华丽光彩。
t_en: Stoneblade lifts his hand up and does a flick. Suddenly, a glorious brilliance flashes before your eyes.

t_ch: 回去检查账户，要是信用点没有减少，我就自愿放弃朋克洛德的身份！别以为你可以在超级骇客的面前为所欲为！
t_en: Go back home and check your account. If you haven't lost any credits, I'll relinquish my Punklordian passport! Don't just think you can get away with doing whatever you want around a super hacker like me!

t_ch: 是我想的那个朋克洛德吗？
t_en: Is that the same Punklorde I think it is?

t_ch: 能培养出我这种超级骇客的星球，恐怕全宇宙都找不到第二个。
t_en: I doubt a super hacker such as me could ever hope to come from any other planet in the universe!

t_ch: 那是个堆满电子废料和快餐盒垃圾的地方，虚拟和现实的界限模糊到快要看不见，涂鸦填满了你能看见的所有墙壁——可到处都是乐趣和冒险。
t_en: It's a place that's filled with e-waste and used takeout packaging. The line between the real and the virtual is so blurry that it's almost invisible, and graffiti covers every wall as far as the eye can see — yet fun and adventure can be found absolutely everywhere.

t_ch: 我爱朋克洛德，同时我也无法抑制住心中快要喷涌而出的叛逆精神，所以才受家族邀请来到这里，帮助建设匹诺康尼。
t_en: I love Punklorde, but I can't contain the rebellious spirit that's always bursting up out of me — so I took up an invitation from The Family to come here and help build Penacony.

t_ch: 但现在，看看这乏味到令人昏睡的会场，或许是到了我该离开的时候了。
t_en: But when I look at this place now, it bores me to death. Maybe the time's come for me to leave.

t_ch: 我对你没有兴趣。
t_en: I'm not interested in you.

t_ch: 哼，那你就走吧。冷漠不过是弱者用来自保的借口罢了。
t_en: Hmph, then go. Apathy is just an excuse the weak use to protect themselves.

t_ch: 我无意针对任何人，但必须得说，会场里进行的那些挑战都极其愚蠢，毫无技巧可言。
t_en: I don't mean to criticize, but I have to say that all the challenges were so stupid that they barely took any skill.

t_ch: 瞧瞧这些可笑的游艺设施，这也叫做挑战？我们朋克洛德的合家欢派对游戏比这有意思多了。
t_en: Just look at these ridiculous amusement facilities. Call that a challenge? Even the block parties on Punklorde are more fun than this.

t_ch: 我爱朋克洛德，同时我也无法抑制住心中快要喷涌而出的叛逆精神，所以才受家族邀请来到这里，帮助建设匹诺康尼。
t_en: I love Punklorde, but I can't contain the rebellious spirit that's always bursting up out of me — so I took up an invitation from The Family to come here and help build Penacony.

t_ch: 但现在，看看这乏味到令人昏睡的会场，或许是到了我该离开的时候了。
t_en: But when I look at this place now, it bores me to death. Maybe the time's come for me to leave.

t_ch: 那个时候啊，有个不知道天高地厚的银头发小姑娘跑来挑战我，哼，我稍微动了动手指，就把她的游戏账号全注销了！
t_en: Back then, there was a silver-haired girl who was full of herself who tried to challenge me. Hmph, with a swipe of one finger I just canceled all of her gaming accounts!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，当我成为超级骇客的时候，朋克洛德所有的论坛都回荡着我的名字。
t_en: Haha, when I first became a super hacker, my nickname shook the forums of Punklorde to the bone.

t_ch: 人们纷纷发送讯息，告知彼此这个重要的消息，内容只有简单的两个字——「「石剑」」！
t_en: Everyone was sending messages to each other about this important news, and they only needed to use one word: "Stoneblade"!

t_ch: 网友们最喜欢复读了。
t_en: There's nothing on the internet more popular than copypasta.

t_ch: …我是真的没听说过。
t_en: ...Really? I've never heard of you.

t_ch: 「石剑」！剑！剑！
t_en: "Stoneblade! Blade! Blade!"

t_ch: 哎，还没完。那个时候啊，有个不知道天高地厚的银头发小姑娘跑来挑战我，哼，我稍微动了动手指，就把她的游戏账号全注销了！
t_en: Ah, I'm not done. Back then, there was a silver-haired girl who was full of herself who tried to challenge me. Hmph, with a swipe of one finger I just cancelled all of her gaming accounts!

t_ch: 你是没见到，那个小姑娘又哭又闹的样子，太好玩了！记得她好像在公司的通缉名单上，名字叫做…银什么来着？银……
t_en: You never saw the way that girl cried and made a scene, it was just hilarious! I remember she had made IPC's most wanted list. What was her name again? Silver-something or Argo-something... Silver...
Silver... hand?

t_ch: 是叫这个吗？感觉不太对劲…反正她还黑进了过庇尔波因特的公司总部，声称要炸掉那里来着。
t_en: Was that the name? Doesn't sound right to me... Anyway, she hacked into Pier Point's HQ before and claimed she was going to blow the whole place up.
Argo... Argenti?

t_ch: 对对，就是这个银枝！她还单挑过塔伊兹育罗斯，打算成为「繁育」令使来着。
t_en: Ah yes, that's the one! Argenti! She also called out Tayzzyronth to try and become an Emanator of the Propagation.

t_ch: …河球棒侠！！
t_en: Silver.... mane guard!?

t_ch: 对对，就是这个银河球棒侠！{F#她}{M#他}还潜入过星穹列车，打算要刺杀阿基维利来着。
t_en: Yes yes, it's this Galactic Baseballer! {F#She}{M#He} even infiltrated the Astral Express before, and plotted to assassinate Akivili.

t_ch: 总得让那些缺乏自知之明的人知道，超级骇客不是谁都能惹的！
t_en: When people are lacking in self-awareness, you gotta let them know not to mess with a super hacker!

t_ch: 我想离开这个无聊的地方，去更广阔的宇宙里冒险。我也想像这些选手一样，站在聚光灯下接受欢呼。但…那终究不是属于我的生活。
t_en: I want to leave this boring place and venture out into the wide open universe. I want to be just like those contestants and stand out in the spotlight as I'm cheered on. But... that's not the life that's been chosen for me.

t_ch: …呜呜呜？
t_en: ...*boohoohoo*?

t_ch: …嘤嘤嘤？
t_en: ...*eep, eep*?

t_ch: 还未等你有反应，「石剑」突然疯狂挥动起手臂来，于是你的视野里出现了一道绚丽的彩虹。
t_en: Before you get a chance to react, Stoneblade suddenly swings his arms around wildly. A brilliant rainbow materializes before your very eyes.

t_ch: 呜。其实…超级骇客的故事，都是骗你的。你不会生气吧？
t_en: *sob*... actually... all those stories about being a super hacker? They were lies. You're not mad at me, are you?

t_ch: 我只是热砂分会场的灯光控制员，平时的工作就是操作这些设备，给现场炒热气氛，致谢名单里都不会出现我的名字，所以才编造了那些经历。
t_en: I'm just the lighting controller at Scorchsand Hall. My work involves operating this equipment and warming up the crowd. You'd never find my name in the credits of anything, so I made all of those events up.

t_ch: 但我听一位朋克洛德宾客跟我讲过「石剑」这位骇客的故事。我羡慕他的自由不羁——所以在这个可以隐去身份的梦境，我借用了他的名字。
t_en: But I heard this story from a Punklordian guest about the great hacker Stoneblade, and I was jealous of that kind of unrestrained freedom — so I decided to borrow his name and use it here in this Dreamscape where one can become anonymous.

t_ch: 唉，我也想像站在聚光灯下，接受观众们的欢呼。但…那终究不是属于我的生活。
t_en: *sigh* I want to be just like those contestants and stand out in the spotlight as I'm cheered on by the audience. But... that's not the life that's been chosen for me.

t_ch: 我喜欢你制造的彩虹。
t_en: I loved the rainbow that you made.

t_ch: 咦…真的吗？
t_en: Huh... really?

t_ch: 所以划走信用点也是…
t_en: So that bit about stealing the credits, too...

t_ch: 嗯…是骗你的。我能做到的，只有改变会场的光线而已。
t_en: Mmm... that was all a lie. All I'm good for is changing up the lighting at a venue.

t_ch: 这是我摸鱼时候研究出来的小技巧，可以通过体感来控制灯光。你看，顺时针挥手是渐暗，逆时针是渐亮……
t_en: This is a little trick I came up with while I was slacking off to control the lights by using hand motions. You see, waving clockwise dims the lights, while counterclockwise brings them up...

t_ch: 「石剑」抓着你絮絮叨叨地讲了好久操控技巧，看得出他确实在这方面很有心得。
t_en: Stoneblade grabs onto you and prattles a never ending spiel about his control panel skills. You can tell that he really does have experience when it comes to this.

t_ch: 呵呵，找个人说说话后感觉好多了…虽然改变不了什么，但还是谢谢你。
t_en: Haha, I feel so much better after talking with someone... even though nothing's actually changed: Thank you very much.

t_ch: …呜呜呜？
t_en: ...*boohoohoo*?

t_ch: …嘤嘤嘤？
t_en: ...*eep, eep*?

t_ch: 还未等你有反应，「石剑」突然疯狂挥动起手臂来，于是你的视野里出现了一道绚丽的彩虹。
t_en: Before you get a chance to react, Stoneblade suddenly swings his arms around wildly. A brilliant rainbow materializes before your very eyes.

t_ch: 呜。其实…超级骇客的故事，都是骗你的。你不会生气吧？
t_en: *sob*... actually... all those stories about being a super hacker? They were lies. You're not mad at me, are you?

t_ch: 我只是热砂分会场的灯光控制员，平时的工作就是操作这些设备，给现场炒热气氛，致谢名单里都不会出现我的名字……
t_en: I'm just the lighting controller at the Scorchsand Hall. My work involves operating this equipment and warming up the crowd. You'll never find my name in the credits of anything...

t_ch: 但我听一位朋克洛德宾客跟我讲过「石剑」这位骇客的故事。我羡慕他的自由不羁——所以在这个可以隐去身份的梦境，我借用了他的名字。
t_en: But I heard this story from a Punklordian guest about the great hacker Stoneblade, and I was jealous of that kind of unrestrained freedom — so I decided to borrow his name and use it here in this Dreamscape where one can become anonymous.

t_ch: 唉，我也想像站在聚光灯下，接受观众们的欢呼。但…那终究不是属于我的生活。
t_en: *sigh* I want to be just like those contestants and stand out in the spotlight as I'm cheered on by the audience. But... that's not the life that's been chosen for me.

t_ch: 我喜欢你制造的彩虹。
t_en: I loved the rainbow that you made.

t_ch: 咦…真的吗？
t_en: Huh... really?

t_ch: 所以黑掉账户也是…
t_en: So all that about hacking accounts was also...

t_ch: 嗯…是骗你的。我能做到的，只有改变会场的光线而已。
t_en: Mmm... that was all a lie. All I'm good for is changing up the lighting at a venue.

t_ch: 这是我摸鱼时候研究出来的小技巧，可以通过体感来控制灯光。你看，顺时针挥手是渐暗，逆时针是渐亮……
t_en: This is a little trick I came up with while I was slacking off to control the lights by using hand motions. You see, waving clockwise dims the lights, while counterclockwise brings them up...

t_ch: 「石剑」抓着你絮絮叨叨地讲了好久操控技巧，看得出他确实在这方面很有心得。
t_en: Stoneblade grabs onto you and prattles a never ending spiel about his control panel skills. You can tell that he really does have experience when it comes to this.

t_ch: 呵呵，找个人说说话后感觉好多了…虽然改变不了什么，但还是谢谢你。
t_en: Haha, I feel so much better after talking with someone... even though nothing's actually changed: Thank you very much.

t_ch: 再这样下去，节目的口碑非得给他们败完了！
t_en: If I keep this up, the ceremony's reputation is going to be ruined by them!

t_ch: 我在热砂分会场工作这么久，遇见这种事还是头一回！
t_en: I've worked at Scorchsand Hall for ages, but this is the first time I've ever seen anything like this!

t_ch: 「那位红发的挑战者，凭借出色的演技和高贵的品格成功征服了所有评委」…就离谱！
t_en: "The redheaded challenger won the hearts of all the judges with incredible acting and superb morals"... What a load of baloney!

t_ch: 什么时候盛典巨星沦为这种只要挤几句煽情的话，就能成功当选的垃圾赛事了？演出实力呢？作为明星的涵养呢？这是选秀，不是比谁的信念更坚定！
t_en: Since when was such a superstar system reduced to a trash competition where anyone who says such pathetic platitudes could win? What about the acting? Where's the celebrity training? This is a talent show, not a contest to see whose faith is stronger!

t_ch: 再这样下去，节目的口碑非得给他们败完了！
t_en: If I keep this up, the ceremony's reputation is going to be ruined by them!

t_ch: 你不是超级骇客吗？
t_en: Aren't you a super hacker?

t_ch: 是不是人设搞错了…
t_en: What's with this guy...

t_ch: 不管！超级骇客也要打工挣钱，我正在气头上，非得要痛骂这群评委，好好出这股恶气！
t_en: So what! Super hackers also have to earn a living, and I'm angry. I'm going to really give these judges a piece of my mind and let off some steam!

t_ch: …好，那么正式开始之前，我们再来对一遍流程：首先是那位冠军的挑战路线，确定记清楚…嗯？哎呀，看来没必要特意去记了……
t_en: ...Alright. Before we officially start, let's go over things once more: First, the winner's route — can you remember all that? ... Ah, looks like you won't have to remember it after all...

t_ch: …啊？可是……
t_en: ...Ah? But...

t_ch: 嗨！你就是{NICKNAME}吧？我是你的粉丝，有空接受个短暂的采访吗？
t_en: Hi! Are you {NICKNAME}, right? I'm one of your fans. Have you got time for a quick interview?

t_ch: 我还有粉丝？
t_en: I have fans now!?

t_ch: 那当然了，就凭你在盛会海选的精彩表现，粉丝还会少吗？
t_en: Of course you do! How could you not, based on your brilliant performance in the Festivity Auditions?

t_ch: 这采访正经吗？
t_en: Is it a serious interview?

t_ch: 放心，我又不会把你骗到薄暮拍卖行去……
t_en: Don't worry. I'd never trick you into going to the Dusk Auction House...

t_ch: 太出名也是种苦恼…
t_en: Who knew being famous could be such a pain...

t_ch: 哈哈，看来你平时走在路上，应该没少被人要签名吧？
t_en: Haha, I bet loads of people bug you for autographs whenever you're just walking around in public, right?

t_ch: 咳、重新介绍一下——我是《逐梦客时讯》驻热砂分会场的记者，目前正制作一期关于新晋冠军，也就是你的节目。
t_en: Ahem, let me reintroduce myself real quick — I'm a reporter from the Dreamchaser Bulletin's Scorchsand Hall section. I'm putting together a piece about the new champion. That's you.

t_ch: 既然能在此时此地遇见，就说明我们之间还挺有缘分的，所以…稍微来聊几句怎么样？
t_en: Seeing as we've managed to meet up here and now, there must be some level of destiny between us. So then... how about a little chit-chat?

t_ch: 这位女士不由分说开始了她的采访。此人谈吐幽默风趣，思路天马行空，却并不教人感到冒犯。
t_en: The lady begins her interview without any further explanation in a humorous style that is freewheeling but never offensive.

t_ch: 你们不知聊了多久，直到她转头对摄影师说——
t_en: You talk and talk for who knows how long, before she turns around to her photographer to say...

t_ch: 好了，今天先到这里吧，辛苦你了。
t_en: Alright. That's it for today. Thank you very much.

t_ch: 也辛苦你啦，{NICKNAME}——那么…既然已经到了下班时间，我能以粉丝的身份请你喝瓶苏乐达吗？
t_en: And thanks to you, {NICKNAME} — so anyway... seeing as it's the end of my shift, can I treat you to a bottle of SoulGlad, as a fan?

t_ch: 出于个人爱好，我时不时会做些海选的攻略，都是些机制的分析和通关技巧的总结。
t_en: As a hobby I sometimes also help contestants out with their strategies, just mechanical analysis and tips on how to clear each stage.

t_ch: 你的经验对我而言，将会是非常宝贵的素材，所以你愿意再和我多聊聊吗？
t_en: Your experience would provide extremely valuable material for a person like me. So would you like to chat a little more with me?

t_ch: 我有些问题想问你。
t_en: I've got some questions to ask you.

t_ch: 当然没问题，你想问什么就只管说，就算是我不知道的，也可以帮你查……
t_en: Of course, no problem. Ask me anything you want. Even if I don't know the answer, I can help you check it out...

t_ch: 介绍下《逐梦客时讯》？
t_en: Can you tell me more about Dreamchaser Bulletin?

t_ch: 嗨，这个啊——其实就是个由逐梦客自发组织创办的期刊，用于进行信息的分享与交换。
t_en: Ah, that — actually it's a self-published magazine from the Dreamchasers who use it to promote and share info.

t_ch: 当人们进入阿斯德纳星系范围内，就能收到《时讯》的订阅推送，不知道你还有没有印象……
t_en: When people enter the Asdana system, they'll get a push notification about subscribing to the bulletin. I wonder if you've had a chance to check it out yet...

t_ch: 总之，期刊的内容非常丰富，不仅有时事头条、悬赏招工这类常规内容，还有不少攻略指南之类的硬通货，你甚至还可以在上面追小说连载。
t_en: Anyway, the magazine is chock-full of rich content — not just things like regular news and vacancies but also lots of hard currency like strategic guides. Or you can even follow the serial fiction in each issue.

t_ch: 我个人建议你订阅一份，这玩意还挺实用的。
t_en: Personally, I suggest you get a subscription — it's actually really useful.

t_ch: 介绍下热砂的时刻？
t_en: Tell me more about the Moment of Scorchsand.

t_ch: 这个嘛…来，深吸口气，你闻到了什么——苏乐达的味道，金钱的味道！
t_en: Oh, that... Come! Take a deep breath, and tell me: What can you smell? The scent of SoulGlad, the aroma of money!

t_ch: 这个时刻中最耀眼的，就是资本用金纸堆出来的明星梦。大家乌泱泱挤作一团，挤破了头去争抢冠军头衔……
t_en: Back then, the most dazzling thing of all was using stacks of gold to build celebrity dreams. Everyone thronged into a bunch, smashing their heads together to fight for the champion title...

t_ch: 这里既是选手的竞技场、也是观众的狂欢之所，更是个巨大的苏乐达广告牌，强势地吸引着所有人的目光。
t_en: This wasn't just the contest arena, but also a party zone for the crowd — and it was also a massive billboard for SoulGlad, drawing in everybody's attention.

t_ch: 先聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's all I wanted to talk about.

t_ch: 唉，愉快的时光总是这么短暂……
t_en: *sigh* Good times never seem to last long...

t_ch: 回见，拜拜。
t_en: Until next time. Later!

t_ch: 没问题，回见！不过已经这个时间了吗…看来今天又要通宵赶稿了……
t_en: No problem. See you later! But it's late already... looks like I'll be pulling another all-nighter again...

t_ch: 说起来，有件事我觉得还是提醒你一下比较好……
t_en: Speaking of which, there's something I think I'd better remind you about...

t_ch: 这届比赛中有位——至少在你这匹黑马杀出来之前——非常有潜力夺冠的种子选手。
t_en: There's one seed in this competition who harbored a lot of winning potential — that is, until a dark horse like you came onto the scene.

t_ch: 你知道的，像我们这样的记者多少有些自己的消息渠道，所以在得知他的存在之后，我找到了他，请求进行赛前采访。
t_en: You know, journalists like us have our own sources, so after learning about his existence, I went looking for him to ask for a pre-match interview.

t_ch: 怎么说呢，这人虽然自负得很，但确实有些实力或是倚仗…我自认好歹算个经验丰富的记者，但从他身上愣是套不出半点有用的消息。
t_en: But — how to put this? Despite being conceited, he does actually have some real strength and background... I thought I was this experienced journalist, but I couldn't manage to get any useful info out of him.

t_ch: 比赛结束之后，我又见到了他，那时候他的状态似乎有些不对劲……
t_en: After the contest finished, I saw him again and something didn't seem quite right about the state he was in...

t_ch: 虽然可能是我多心了，但出于谨慎，你最好还是留意一下那个叫做加西亚的男人。
t_en: I might just be paranoid, but out of an abundance of caution, I suggest you keep one eye open for this Garcia guy.

t_ch: 虽然可能是我多心了，但出于谨慎，你最好还是留意一下那个叫做加西亚的男人。
t_en: I might just be paranoid, but out of an abundance of caution, I suggest you keep one eye open for this Garcia guy.

t_ch: 怎么就连她都退圈了……
t_en: How did she even end up getting kicked out?

t_ch: …啊…抱歉，我走神了，我只是稍微有点…迷茫？
t_en: ...Ah ...Sorry, I was a little distracted there. I just got a little... confused?

t_ch: 你知道吗，免费攻略这种东西，本身就是为爱发电的产物。我原本非常享受这种将所知信息汇总整理，推导出个相对正确的结论，然后分享给大家的过程。
t_en: You know, these free strategies of mine are a total labor of love. I just really enjoy this process of gathering all the information, proposing a probable hypothesis, and then sharing it with everyone.

t_ch: 我也一直知道，但凡涉及到流量和利益相关，某些人的手段能有多下作…啊，抱歉，我现在思路有些混乱，我只是想说——
t_en: And I've always known just how low some people can go when it comes to numbers and profits... er, sorry — I'm a little muddle-headed right now. I just think that...

t_ch: 网络可真是个大泥坑，坑里那些泥鳅，就喜欢将你的过失无限放大、对你的言行恶意解读，想尽办法溅你一身泥水。
t_en: The internet really is a giant cesspool, with pesky trolls there blowing up your mistakes a thousandfold, uncharitably twisting your words and deeds as they try their best to drag you down into the mud with them.

t_ch: 那位前辈的作品明明那么用心、那么考究，她为每一篇攻略耗费的心血我都看在眼里，可偏偏只要水军带带风向，舆论就完全一边倒了……
t_en: My senior's work has been so meticulous and well-researched, and I've seen for myself how much effort she puts into every one of her strategies — but as soon as the bots start coming, public opinion drastically tilts out of her favor...

t_ch: 要求一位免费攻略的创作者拥有崇高的道德、纵览全局的眼界…你说这事好不好笑？
t_en: Demanding the creator of a free strategy to have upstanding morals and wide-reaching vision... isn't that hilarious?

t_ch: 算了，早点退圈也好，有这个时间和精力不如去做一些更有意义的事，何必为了这些浪费生命……
t_en: Whatever. It's good to get kicked out early so that you can put that time and energy into doing something a little more meaningful. Why waste your life just for that...?

t_ch: 放下碗骂厨子…有些人就真的不配吃饭！
t_en: Beggars can't be choosers... Some people really don't deserve nice things!

t_ch: 我就不明白了，大家都是成年人，为自己的行为负责就那么难吗？
t_en: What I don't get, though, is that we're all adults — is it really so hard to take responsibility for your actions?

t_ch: 看到不喜欢的攻略就不看呗，非得在评论区找存在感，还向家族举报，缺不缺德啊？
t_en: You don't have to read a strategy if you don't like it. Why you gotta go posting on message boards, seeking attention — or snitching to The Family? It's wrong, isn't it?

t_ch: 人家做考据攻略你喷背景介绍太多、做玩法攻略又嫌干巴巴没内容，和预想中的不同就是质量不行、自己闯关没过就逮着出攻略的人骂。
t_en: When you put out a guide with ample research, some people will inevitably think the intro is too long. If you do a gameplay strategy, it's boring and lacking in content. If anything differs from expectations, that's a quality problem — and you'll end up with your head on a stake if someone tries to blitz a map but cannot clear it.

t_ch: 整天鸡蛋里挑骨头，自己偏偏还不会下蛋，也不知道哪来的优越感。
t_en: Just being super picky and fussy all day and all night without actually having any skill of your own — who knows where this sense of superiority comes from?

t_ch: 还有些一看人创作者出名了，就热火朝天地挖黑料好证明对方「德不配位」，怕不是在梦境里长时间见不到太阳，内心阴暗到看什么都是黑的。
t_en: And then there're those people who gleefully dig up scandalous histories to try and cancel someone as soon as they learn they're famous. These people have either been kept out of the sun too long inside Dreamscapes, or they're so depressed that they only see pessimism everywhere they look.

t_ch: 智慧生命的多样性，可真是让人大开眼界……
t_en: The diversity of intelligent life really is utterly eye-opening...

t_ch: 说实话，我对你身上的秘密真的非常好奇……
t_en: Honestly, I'm really curious to hear about some of your secrets...

t_ch: 这么说或许有些冒昧，但你和你的同伴在「戏梦奇战」中的表演，其实并不那么…出彩。
t_en: I might be speaking out of turn by saying so, but what you and your team did for the Dreamplay Fantasia performance wasn't exactly... out of this world.

t_ch: 我承认那很有趣，但远不如一些专业艺人那般打动人心。说实话，当时我并不觉得你们能够通过。
t_en: I admit, it was lots of fun — but it was nowhere near as impressive as some of the other pros. Honestly, I didn't even think you were going to pass.

t_ch: 而评委们的反应着实出乎了我的预料——他们的表现过于真情实感，完全看不出任何黑箱操作的迹象。
t_en: But the judges' reactions went far beyond my wildest imagination — they were so emotional. I couldn't see any signs at all of black-box tampering.

t_ch: 而后来通过我那微不足道的人脉，也确定了你们并没有在流程上作弊。
t_en: Later on, I even asked some of my meager connections about it, and they all confirmed that you guys didn't cheat.

t_ch: 所以我在想，你是不是有某种特殊能力，就比如能影响别人的心情、改变别人的想法之类的……
t_en: So I'm thinking perhaps you have some kind of special ability, like influencing people's mood or somehow getting people to change their minds...

t_ch: 当然，我只好奇而已，并没有打算窥探你的隐私。毕竟每个人都有自己的小秘密，尤其是像你这样的神秘无名客，对吧？
t_en: Of course, I'm just curious about all of this — I don't mean to intrude. After all, everyone has their own secrets, especially an enigmatic Nameless like yourself, right?

t_ch: …群演！群演呢？下个环节的群演都跑哪去了？！
t_en: ...Extras! Hey, extras? Where's the extras for the next item?

t_ch: 气死我了，气死我了！为了这么个小节目请了一大帮子执行导演，没想到个顶个的都是关系户，干不了一点活的废物！
t_en: I'm so angry, just livid! I hired a small army of executive directors just for this little skit. I never thought that they'd all be useless good-for-nothings scratching each other's backs while they do nothing all day!

t_ch: 一阵刺耳的哨声响起，像极了机动球赛场边情绪激动的观众们制造出来的噪音。你被吓得一激灵，四下寻找发出噪声的源头——
t_en: There's a piercing screech, like the screams of a hyped-up crowd at a Roboball match. You tremble with fear and scour your surroundings for the source of the noise...

t_ch: ——然后你发现，发出那<unbreak>180</unbreak>分贝尖锐哨声的正是面前这位暴躁的智械女士。
t_en: ...before realizing that this piercing <unbreak>180</unbreak>-decibel scream is actually coming from the cranky Intellitron lady before you.

t_ch: …你盯着我看干嘛？等等…莫非你也是那群废物请来的群演？
t_en: ...What are you staring at? Hang on... Don't tell me you're one of the extras those losers hired?

t_ch: 怎么就你一个？其他人都跑哪儿去了？
t_en: Why is it just you? Where has everyone else gone?

t_ch: 你看我长得像群演吗？
t_en: Do I look like an extra to you?

t_ch: 哎唷，口气还不小呐？告诉你吧，只要你领的还是<unbreak>1200</unbreak>苜蓿币一天的工钱，哪怕你美若天仙也只是个小群演！
t_en: Oh, you sure talk a big game. Let me tell you something: As long as you're getting paid a daily rate of <unbreak>1200</unbreak> Alfalfa credits, I don't care if you're Aphrodite herself — you're still just a lowly extra!

t_ch: 我可是主角。
t_en: I'm the lead.

t_ch: 哎唷，口气还不小呐？哪部作品找你当主角，制片人怕不是要把家里的地板都给赔光了吧？
t_en: Oh, you sure talk a big game — who picked you to be their lead? That producer's gonna lose their shirt, let alone all their worldly possessions.

t_ch: 你这{F#披头散发}{M#蓬头垢面}的像什么话，赶紧去后台找妆造收拾收拾！别花太久，要是赶不上下轮开镜的时间，就别想领工钱了！
t_en: You {F#ugly duckling}{M#slobby wretch}, get backstage and find a make-up artist right now! And hurry up about it: If you're not back before the next scene, you can kiss your wages goodbye!

t_ch: 容我和你说两句。
t_en: May I please have a word with you?

t_ch: 聊天？我可没那个闲情逸致！
t_en: You want to chat? I'm not in the mood for that!

t_ch: 红哨小姐用她体内的某个机械器官发出了一阵聒噪的哨声。你决定不理会她恶劣的态度，自顾自地继续提问。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle lets out a harsh squeal from a mechanical component somewhere inside her. You decide to ignore her bad attitude and plunge straight on with your questions.

t_ch: 你们在拍什么节目？
t_en: What are you shooting?

t_ch: 你好歹也算个演员，居然连自己参演的节目叫什么名字都不知道？就凭你这点敬业精神，这辈子都别想混上个有名有姓的角！
t_en: So you're an actor but you don't even know the name of the show you're in? You'll never ever land a big role if that's your sense of professionalism!

t_ch: 我们在拍的这档节目叫《翻滚吧！银河球棒侠》，是受银河间行侠仗义的「无名客」启发创办的一档益智冒险类综艺节目，面向的人群是……
t_en: We're shooting a show called Jump! Galactic Baseballer and it's an adventure-puzzle variety show inspired by these intergalactic heroes "The Nameless." Its target demographic is...

t_ch: …我和你说那么多干嘛，反正你拍完两个镜头就要换下个剧组了吧？走吧走吧，别干扰我工作！
t_en: ...Why am I telling you all this? You're just going to shoot two scenes and then switch crews, right? Come on. Let's move — and stop interfering with my job!

t_ch: 红哨小姐发出一声沉闷、烦躁的哨音。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle lets out a depressed, cranky whistle.

t_ch: 不瞒你说，我可是盛会巨星。
t_en: To be honest with you, I won Festive Superstar.

t_ch: 红哨小姐发出一声短促、轻蔑的哨音。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle lets out a sharp, disdainful whistle.

t_ch: 呵呵，那我还是机械飞升之后的梅芙恩•伊里斯呢。少来这套！
t_en: Ha, sure — and I'm Maeven Ellis after her mechanical ascension. Pull the other one!

t_ch: 你吹那么大声干嘛？
t_en: Why are you screaming so loud?

t_ch: 怎么，这就嫌吵了？
t_en: Why, am I being noisy?

t_ch: 教你一招，等会儿开拍的时候，你找准那几个明星的机位，对着它们拼命做鬼脸——到时候你就知道什么叫震耳欲聋的哨声了！
t_en: I'll teach you a life hack. Once we start shooting, find out which cameras the celebs will be facing, and pull faces at them. You'll soon know the meaning of a loud scream!

t_ch: 红哨小姐发出一声刺耳、威胁的哨音。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle lets out a piercing, menacing whistle.

t_ch: 不烦你了。
t_en: I'll stop bothering you.

t_ch: 算你识相。记住，我最讨厌别人打扰我工作！
t_en: If you know what's good for you. Remember, I hate people interfering with my job!

t_ch: 不烦你了。
t_en: I'll stop bothering you.

t_ch: 算你识相。记住，我最讨厌别人打扰我工作！
t_en: If you know what's good for you. Remember, I hate people interfering with my job!

t_ch: 红哨小姐体内的某个机械器官发出了一阵聒噪的哨声，充分代表了她此时的心境。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle lets out a harsh squeal from a mechanical component somewhere inside her, which vividly represents her current state of mind.

t_ch: 开拍开拍！谁再耽误了拍摄时间，小心我当场炒他鱿鱼！
t_en: ACTION! If anyone else gets in the way of this shoot, you'll be fired on the spot!

t_ch: 红哨小姐体内的某个机械器官发出了一阵聒噪的哨声，充分代表了她此时的心境。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle lets out a harsh squeal from a mechanical component somewhere inside her, which vividly represents her current state of mind.

t_ch: 呵…什么盛会巨星，根本就是个噱头！
t_en: Ha... what do you mean by "Festive Superstar"? That's just a gimmick!

t_ch: 告诉你吧，在匹诺康尼举办的这些个比赛、选秀…就没有一个不是被内定了结果的！
t_en: Let me tell you something, there isn't a single contest or show in Penacony that isn't rigged!

t_ch: 有一个算一个，全都是剧本！不知道从什么时候开始，家族根本就容不得半点「计划之外」的故事情节出现了！
t_en: Every single one of them — they're all scripted! The Family has been turning down any stories that are "outside the plan" for who knows how long!

t_ch: 想稍微搞点节目效果，方案立马就会被家族的人给否决掉！从什么时候开始，匹诺康尼这地方连一点小小的灰色幽默都容不下了？
t_en: The Family will always veto anyone trying to drop a little drama into their role. And for ages, Penacony has become the kind of place where not even a little gray humor is tolerated.

t_ch: 越想越气…还是工作吧！忙起来就没时间瞎想了。
t_en: Thinking about it just enrages me... I should get back to work! My mind can't race if it's busy.

t_ch: 红哨小姐平静了下来。对于熟悉她的人来说，这称得上是个奇迹。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle calms down, which is a miracle if you ask anyone who knows her.

t_ch: 休息五分钟——该喝水的喝水，该加油的加油。
t_en: Take five — rehydrate and refuel if you need it.

t_ch: 红哨小姐平静了下来。对于熟悉她的人来说，这称得上是个奇迹。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle calms down, which is a miracle if you ask anyone who knows her.

t_ch: 安装这个哨子模组是我这辈子做过的最正确的决定。
t_en: Installing this whistle module was the best decision I'd ever made.

t_ch: 只要哨子一响，全世界的人都能立马进入状态，根本用不着我多费口舌。这样一来，我的语言模组的故障率就大大减小了，使用寿命也能延长好几倍。
t_en: One blow of the whistle and everyone just falls into place without me having to waste my breath. That reduces my speech module's failure rate and stretches out its lifespan several times over.

t_ch: 不过也有坏处。时间久了，片场的所有人都只会用「红哨小姐」这个外号称呼我了…哪怕是我身边的人，知道我真名的也没有几个。
t_en: However, it has its drawbacks. After a while, everybody started calling me "Miss Red Whistle"... Not even the people in my team knew my real name.

t_ch: 伊丽莎白——我的名字叫伊丽莎白。奇怪，我为什么要把真名告诉你？我们也不熟啊……
t_en: Elizabeth — my name is Elizabeth. That's weird, why am I telling you my real name? We barely know each other...

t_ch: …算了，无伤大雅。只要能把活干好，你想怎么称呼我都行。
t_en: ...Whatever, no harm no foul. Call me whatever you like, as long as you get the job done.

t_ch: 休息时间快结束了…喝口水，歇一下，然后赶紧去准备下一组镜头吧。
t_en: Break time's almost over... Drink up and catch some rest, then get ready for the next few scenes.

t_ch: 红哨小姐平静了下来。对于熟悉她的人来说，这称得上是个奇迹。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle calms down, which is a miracle if you ask anyone who knows her.

t_ch: 安装这个哨子模组是我这辈子做过的最正确的决定。
t_en: Installing this whistle module was the best decision I'd ever made.

t_ch: 只要哨子一响，全世界的人都能立马进入状态，根本用不着我多费口舌。这样一来，我的语言模组的故障率就大大减小了，使用寿命也能延长好几倍。
t_en: One blow of the whistle and everyone just falls into place without me having to waste my breath. That reduces my speech module's failure rate and stretches out its lifespan several times over.

t_ch: 不过也有坏处。时间久了，片场的所有人都只会用「红哨小姐」这个外号称呼我了…哪怕是我身边的人，知道我真名的也没有几个。
t_en: However, it has its drawbacks. After a while, everybody started calling me "Miss Red Whistle"... Not even the people in my team knew my real name.

t_ch: 伊丽莎白——我的名字叫伊丽莎白。奇怪，我为什么要把真名告诉你？我们也不熟啊……
t_en: Elizabeth — my name is Elizabeth. That's weird, why am I telling you my real name? We barely know each other...

t_ch: …算了，无伤大雅。只要能把活干好，你想怎么称呼我都行。
t_en: ...Whatever, no harm no foul. Call me whatever you like, as long as you get the job done.

t_ch: 红哨小姐发出一声低频、沉闷的哨音，她的心情似乎有些低落。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle lets out a low, depressed whistle. She seems to be down.

t_ch: 谁不想当大导演啊？光有那份心还不够，还得有那个命才行……
t_en: Who doesn't want to be a famous director? It's not enough to have just the talent, though — you need luck as well...

t_ch: 红哨小姐发出一声低频、沉闷的哨音，她的心情似乎有些低落。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle lets out a low, depressed whistle. She seems to be down.

t_ch: 唉…想当年，我可是以专业第一的成绩从折纸大学的导演研修班毕业的。
t_en: *sigh*... I remember when I graduated as a valedictorian from the directors' program at Paperfold University.

t_ch: 谁年轻的时候还没个当大导演的梦想？当时的我胸怀大志，发誓要在匹诺康尼高手云集的电影界打出一片自己的天地……
t_en: Who didn't dream of being a famous director when they were a kid? Back then, I was hungry for success and vowed that I'd make my way in the competitive cinema scene in Penacony and make a name for myself...

t_ch: …怎奈造化弄人！刚进克劳克影业的前两年，我老实得像个白痴，拍了很多老牌制片人丢过来的边角料剧本…我还以为那是正常新人「积攒经验」的过程。
t_en: ...but the fates make fools of even the best-laid plans! During my first two years at Clock Studios, I was so honest and naive to a fault, shooting all the scrap scripts that seasoned producers can't wait to wash their hands off of... I thought that's the normal process for rookies to build up experience.

t_ch: 没成想，那些早期的作品居然会变成贯穿我整个职业生涯的黑历史！从我决定接下那些剧本的一刻起，成为大导演的理想就和我彻底无缘了……
t_en: I never thought that those early shows would become a stain on my entire career path! From the moment I decided to take them on, my dream of becoming a great director was over...

t_ch: 唉…如果有可能的话，我真想放下这些无趣的场务工作，继续去追逐自己的导演梦啊……
t_en: *sigh*... What would I give to just let go of all this boring production assistant work and instead keep chasing my dream of becoming a director...

t_ch: 红哨小姐连续发出一阵短促、欢快的哨音，连片场的空气中都飘满了她对工作的热爱。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle lets out a series of short, cheery whistles. Her passion for work seems to even permeate the very vibe of the film set.

t_ch: 发号施令的快乐，一般人根本想象不到！哈！
t_en: It's unimaginable for most people! This joy of dishing out orders! Hah!

t_ch: 红哨小姐连续发出一阵短促、欢快的哨音，片场的空气中充斥着热爱的香气。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle lets out a series of short, cheery whistles. A sweet smell of enthusiasm fills the film set.

t_ch: 要说这份工作最让人神清气爽的地方，大概就是很能满足人的掌控欲了吧——只要我想，我可以依照个人心情随意调度现场的每一个人、每一件道具！
t_en: If you ask me, the best thing about this job is how it satisfies a person's inner control freak: I can rearrange any actor or any prop on set however I want just based on how I'm feeling!

t_ch: 苏乐达瓶放歪了？重新摆！场务有人在偷懒？狠狠骂！
t_en: Is there a bottle of SoulGlad placed at the wrong angle? Reposition it! Is one of the crew slacking off? Chew them out!

t_ch: 只要是在节目拍摄期间，我就是说话最好使的人！哪怕是家主亲临片场，也得等这条过了再说！
t_en: So long as it's during a shoot, whatever I say goes! Even if any family head wanted to drop in, they still have to wait for me!

t_ch: 啊，心情真好…搞得我都想去黄金的时刻玩上两圈了！只可惜拍摄任务太重……
t_en: Ah, I'm in such a good mood... so much so that I'm down to play a couple rounds in the Golden Hour! Shame I have all these shooting tasks to get done...

t_ch: 你看着还挺闲的，那这几个艾迪恩硬币你拿去吧——记得替我多赢点奖品！
t_en: You seem to have plenty of time on your hands. How about you take a few of these Aideen tokens with you — and try to win me as many prizes as you can!

t_ch: 红哨小姐连续发出一阵短促、欢快的哨音，片场的空气中充斥着热爱的香气。
t_en: Miss Red Whistle lets out a series of short, cheery whistles. A sweet smell of enthusiasm fills the film set.

t_ch: 要说这份工作最让人神清气爽的地方，大概就是很能满足人的掌控欲了吧——只要我想，我可以依照个人心情随意调度现场的每一个人、每一件道具！
t_en: If you ask me, the best thing about this job is how it satisfies a person's inner control freak: I can rearrange any actor or any prop on set however I want just based on how I'm feeling!

t_ch: 苏乐达瓶放歪了？重新摆！场务有人在偷懒？狠狠骂！
t_en: Is there a bottle of SoulGlad placed at the wrong angle? Reposition it! Is one of the crew slacking off? Chew them out!

t_ch: 只要是在节目拍摄期间，我就是说话最好使的人！哪怕是家主亲临片场，也得等这条过了再说！
t_en: So long as it's during a shoot, whatever I say goes! Even if any family head wanted to drop in, they still have to wait for me!

t_ch: …新来的？先前在流梦礁没见过你啊。
t_en: ...Are you a newbie? I haven't seen you around the Dreamflux Reef before.

t_ch: 喂。你叫什么名字，是从哪来的，到这个地方想做什么？别想糊弄我，这把盗贼的匕首见识过太多谎言，每个都得到了他们应有的下场。
t_en: You. What's your name? Where'd you come from? Why're you here? Don't try anything funny, my bandit dagger has seen enough liars and they've all got what was coming for them.

t_ch: 没听过银河球棒侠的威名吗？
t_en: Have you not heard of the great Galactic Baseballer?

t_ch: 没有。那是啥？我知道巡海游侠、路灯侠、垃圾桶侠，甚至还有广告牌侠，可这球棒侠还是第一次听说，不会是你瞎编的吧？
t_en: No. What's that? I've heard of Galaxy Rangers, Street Lamp Man, Trashcan Man, even Billboard Man... I've never heard of this Baseballer. Are you just making this up?

t_ch: 虽说这梦里会动的物件到处都是，但球棒是真没见过。
t_en: I know dreams are full of random objects that move and talk, but I've never seen a baseball bat before.

t_ch: 梦里还会有盗贼？
t_en: There are bandits even in a dream?

t_ch: 哈，你问了个好问题。
t_en: Hah, a fine question.

t_ch: 我刚到这的时候，也尝试过重拾老本行。可没过多久我就想通了，就算夺来了东西，也没法带回现实啊，那何必还要冒这个风险？
t_en: When I first got here, I tried picking up my old profession. But it didn't take me long to realize that I couldn't take any of the stuff I pilfered back to the real world. So, what's the point of taking that risk?

t_ch: 所以我现在就躺平咯。在这地方和大伙聊聊天，唠唠家族的闲话，也挺舒服的。
t_en: So, I'm out of the game now. I'm pretty cozy just chatting with people around here and gossiping about The Family.

t_ch: 好啦。我算是看出来了，你不是家族派来的人，身上没那股恶心的同谐味。
t_en: Alright. I guess I can tell you're not someone The Family sent here. You don't have that foul stench of Harmony on you.

t_ch: 最近莫名其妙出现的人有点多，我得提防点，要是混进来几只猎犬就麻烦了。流梦礁里都是群想过安静生活的人，不愿意被外面那些破事打扰。
t_en: There have been a lot of people appearing out of nowhere here lately, I need to keep my guard up. I can't afford to have the Bloodhounds finding their way in here. The Dreamflux Reef is a place full of people who just want to live a peaceful life. They don't want to be disturbed by the chaos of the outside world.

t_ch: 忘了自我介绍。我叫柯洛达，是个…嘿，星际牛仔！
t_en: I never introduced myself. My name is Cloda, and I'm a... heh, interstellar cowgirl!

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 行啊，你要和我聊点什么？
t_en: Alright, what do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 你为什么会在流梦礁？
t_en: Why are you here in the Dreamflux Reef?

t_ch: 哎呀，这个说来话长。跟大部分人差不多，眼睛一黑，醒来就被带到这了。
t_en: Ah, that's a long story. Much like the majority of people here, I blacked out and woke up here.

t_ch: 只不过呢…我进梦境的方式有点特殊。你们宾客都是通过酒店入梦的吧，但我不一样，借助的是塔利亚的一种自制装置，原理讲不太清。
t_en: Although... I entered the Dreamscape a little differently than most people do. Guests like you came in through the hotel, right? I didn't. I used a custom Talian device. I can't really explain how it works.

t_ch: 进来还算容易，想出去可就麻烦了。当我发现怎么都离不开这个倒霉的梦时，就只能认命了。
t_en: Getting in was easy enough, but it's getting out that is the challenge. When I realized I could never leave this unfortunate dream, I just had to accept my fate.

t_ch: 你可以在「黄金的时刻」生活。
t_en: You could live in the Golden Hour?

t_ch: 那个挤满人的地方？不行不行，我最受不了这种虚荣浮夸的地方，明明都是普通人，非得装成高贵的模样，做给谁看呢！
t_en: Where it's crowded with people? Absolutely not. I can't stand fake and conceited places like that. They're all just normal people, yet they pretend like they're all rich and elegant nobles... Who are they trying to impress?

t_ch: 你可以在「朝露公馆」生活。
t_en: You could live in the Dewlight Pavilion?

t_ch: 哈？你不会在搞笑吧。那种严肃的地方，住久了绝对会变成疯子！
t_en: Huh? Are you joking? That place is so serious and strict that I'd end up going insane there!

t_ch: 还是流梦礁适合我啊。自由，清净，没什么麻烦事。爽快！
t_en: I think the Dreamflux Reef suits me best. Free, peaceful, and free of trouble. It's refreshing!

t_ch: …你会做青椒肉丝吗？
t_en: ...Do you know how to cook pepper steak?

t_ch: 这是什么匹诺康尼时兴的开玩笑方式吗？为什么你也要问啊。
t_en: Is that some kind of Penaconian joke that I don't understand? Why would you ask that?

t_ch: ——统一回复：不会，我根本不知道这是啥！
t_en: ...My straight response: No, I don't even know what it is!

t_ch: 我是不是在哪见过你？
t_en: Have we met somewhere?

t_ch: 有吗？我对你倒是完全没印象，话说…你该不会是在通缉令上看到的吧？
t_en: Have you? I don't remember you at all. Say... I wasn't on a wanted poster, was I?

t_ch: 快想想。这个很重要。
t_en: Think quickly. This is very important.

t_ch: 好像是张通缉令…
t_en: I think it was a wanted poster...

t_ch: 不会吧！我都好久没开张过了，怎么通缉令还保留着。不应该啊？
t_en: No way! I've been out of business for ages. How are there still wanted posters around? That can't be right.

t_ch: 好像有个广告牌…
t_en: I think I saw you on a billboard...

t_ch: 广告牌？「黄金的时刻」追着人跑的那种？
t_en: A billboard? Like the ones in the Golden Hour that chase after people?

t_ch: 为什么广告牌上面会有我的形象？这也太奇怪了吧。不可能，绝对不可能！
t_en: Why would I be on a billboard? That's not possible. There's no way!

t_ch: 它让我带句话给你。
t_en: It gave me a message for you.

t_ch: 「有只广告牌在中央车站前等你，它会一直等下去的！」
t_en: "There is a billboard in front of the Golden Central Station waiting for you. It will always be waiting for you!"

t_ch: …什么意思？广告牌…等我干嘛？是我欠了它的钱吗？莫名其妙的。
t_en: ...What does that mean? Why would a billboard be waiting for me...? Do I owe it money or something? That's so strange.
